claims.claim: Excited about zero-knowledge & verifiable computation (SNARKs/zkVMs), spending the most engineering time on Jolt as the ZK frontier
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about Ethereum L2 scaling (rollups, proto-danksharding/4844, OP-stack superchain) as the precondition for cheap blockspace
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about fully on-chain games as composable, credibly-neutral worlds (EVE/CCP, L2-native titles)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about AI × crypto: provenance/attestation, sybil-resistant proof-of-personhood, and machine ownership/transacting
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about crypto payments & a frontend UX reset (stablecoins, smart accounts, passkeys, embedded wallets) routing around the platform tax
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about decentralized social / open networks (Farcaster) as a neutral, unclosable substrate
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned that memecoins / the casino are not technically interesting and set the industry back
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned about token-first thinking / 'tokenomics' pie-chart consulting; a token serves the protocol, not the goal
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned about Bitcoin's long-term security model (fee vs. subsidy) and lack of programmability cutting it off from ZK
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Optimistic on ETH as the native asset/network resource of the leading smart-contract platform with a compounding scaling roadmap (fundamentals, not a price call)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Admires MKR (Maker) as the cleanest open-protocol-design example and the risk token that underwrites the system
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Risk-on builder's posture: 'skeptical in analysis, optimistic in action'; next-gen infra is online and there's never been a better time to build
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Major new focus on DAO governance design and the Compound 'governance attack' (Aug 2024) — timing games, private voting (Cicada), veto councils — plus championing the DUNA legal framework for DAOs; not anticipated by the prediction.

Open-source AI advocacy (Jul 2024): explicit stance that open/uncensored models have caused no identifiable harm and that closed source harms innovation and safety — an AI-policy position beyond the crypto-provenance frame.
Promotion of formal-verification / symbolic-testing tooling (Halmos for the EVM, used on Uniswap v4) as a security priority — a new emphasis.
Concrete skeptical-analysis work on honest crypto metrics: 'active addresses' is gameable; estimating ~30–60M real monthly users vs 220M addresses — a measurement-rigor stance.

Intensified embrace of AI agents as autonomous onchain economic actors (truth_terminal, TEEs / 'decentralized autonomous chatbots', AIs custodying wallets and paying for their own compute) — an extension well beyond generic 'machine ownership'.
Sharpened position that light clients (Helios, multichain/OP-Stack) are 'fundamental to Ethereum scaling' and rollup interoperability.
notes: Nearly the entire prediction is reaffirmed in the window. The Aug 2024 token talk directly restates the protocol-first / token-as-tool concern and the Maker-as-clean-design view; Jolt, L2/blob-target advocacy, on-chain games (Proof of Play, EVE Frontier), proof-of-personhood/machine-ownership, and stablecoin payments are all explicitly confirmed, as is the constructive risk-on builder posture (the optimistic 2024 State of Crypto report paired with rigorous metric skepticism). No claim is contradicted. The only un-addressed claim is the Bitcoin security/programmability concern: he touches Bitcoin's 'extreme ossification' and says 'it's incredible that we have bitcoin,' but never restates the security-model-must-change or ZK-programmability worry, so it's scored absent. The big un-anticipated additions are a heavy DAO-governance/DUNA thread and an open-source-AI advocacy stance.
